actionable_feedback: Attach the isolation_calibration_audit.xlsx file with cells that contain the actual formulas (not only values) so reviewers can inspect formulas and cell references.

If a .xlsx cannot be provided, include an executable Jupyter notebook or script that reproduces the workbook computations and writes a human-readable artifact (CSV + formula metadata) and attach its output.

Add a row_level_citation_mapping artifact that maps each spreadsheet row/cell to the exact source (task.json:R1..R4) and include a one-line proof (cell address -> source key) for every computed cell referenced in the memo (e.g., Packet!H2 -> task.json:R1.measured_kohm).

Include a requirements.txt (or embed pip install instructions) listing needed libraries (openpyxl and/or xlsxwriter) and a short reproducibility README with exact commands to run in a clean environment.

Explicitly enumerate and justify any threshold or assumption values used in the workbook (e.g., nominal/watch/critical thresholds), and mark those cells in the workbook with comments or a dedicated 'Assumptions' sheet.

Re-run the generation step in CI or an isolated environment and attach the stdout/stderr showing successful workbook creation; if third-party libs are unavailable, add a fallback (CSV + notebook) and document it.
artifact_specific_comments: safety_case_note.md: Complete and internally consistent. It documents formulas, sheets, sensitivity checks, and remediation scenarios; this is high quality and matches actual_outputs metadata.

isolation_calibration_audit.xlsx: Referenced in actual_outputs.original_fields but not verifiable in previews. The absence of a materialized workbook reduces reproducibility confidence-attach the file with formulas visible.
feedback_summary: The safety_case_note.md is comprehensive and well-structured (scope, inputs, formulas, decision logic, sensitivity). However, end-to-end reproducibility is not verifiable because the claimed workbook artifact (.xlsx) with visible formulas was not materialized/accessible in the provided artifacts and the trace records a ModuleNotFoundError for openpyxl when attempting .xlsx generation. Provide the workbook or an executable alternative plus environment requirements and an explicit row-level citation mapping so reviewers can re-run or inspect formulas directly.

grader_limitations: Semantic verification limited by lack of accessible spreadsheet artifact and a failed dependency when generating .xlsx in the trace.
required_actions: Attach isolation_calibration_audit.xlsx with visible formulas or an executable notebook/script.
Provide explicit row_level_citation_mapping artifact and a short change log.
If external libraries are required, include a requirements.txt or embed calculation code to ensure reproducibility.
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Verifier flagged missing/executable workbook content and OpenPyXL failure; otherwise artifact contract passed.
